svg.text: ਪੁਰਾਤਨ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ · A Map of the Puratan Sikh Granths
Dasam Granth · Sarabloh Granth · Suraj Prakash · Prem Sumarag · Sau Sakhi — and the gupt banis of the dals

Sri Guru Granth Sahib Ji alone is the Guru. These granths are the treasured literature of the Guru’s darbar and the Panth — the Tenth Master’s bani, history, code, poetry and prophecy around the throne of Gurbani.

g.text: compiled by Guru Arjan Dev Ji
compiled by Baba Deep Singh Ji
rahit and prophecy of the Khalsa age

svg.text: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਦਸਮ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ ਸਾਹਿਬ · Sri Dasam Granth Sahib
The bani of Guru Gobind Singh Ji — the granth you already recite every day

g.text: Braj, Punjabi & Persian in Gurmukhi · 1,428 angs in the standard sarup
beside Guru Granth Sahib Ji at Takht Hazur Sahib, Takht Patna Sahib & in the Nihang dals

svg.text: Inside: Jaap Sahib · Akal Ustat (“recognise all humanity as one”) · Bachittar Natak — the Guru’s own autobiography · Chandi Charitras &
Chandi di Var (the first pauri of Ardas) · Gyan Prabodh · Chaubis Avtar · Shabad Hazare · 33 Savaiye · Khalsa Mahima · Zafarnama.
Your nitnem’s Jaap Sahib, Tav-Prasad Savaiye and Chaupai Sahib are Dasam bani — as are the banis of the amrit sanchar itself.
➜ Start here: Jaap Sahib with translation, then Bachittar Natak — the most gripping entry point in Sikh literature.
ਸ੍ਰੀ ਸਰਬਲੋਹ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ · Sarabloh Granth
“The Granth of All-Steel” — also called Manglacharan Puran

g.text: Guru Gobind Singh Ji, in the darbar at Nanded
Braj with Sanskritic vocabulary, Gurmukhi script
5 chapters; Chapter 1 alone = 753 verses in dozens of metres
Nihang dals (Buddha Dal) & Takht Hazur Sahib

svg.text: Inside: a vast cosmic epic — the battle of the devas against the demon Bhimnad —
framing meditations on Akal Purakh as All-Steel (sarab-loh), the deathless power
behind all forms. Home of the famous Khalsa Mahima: “ਖ਼ਾਲਸਾ ਮੇਰੋ ਰੂਪ ਹੈ ਖ਼ਾਸ —
the Khalsa is my own special form.”
Why it matters: the theological heart of the Khalsa’s warrior identity — shastar as
a form of the Divine — and of the sarbloh bibek discipline of the Nihang Singhs.
➜ Start here: the Khalsa Mahima, then Chapter 1 with a satik (translation).
Entry effort: ●●●●○ (dense Braj–Sanskrit poetry — use a satik)
ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਸੂਰਜ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ · Suraj Prakash
“The Splendour of the Sun of the Gurus’ Glory”

g.text: ~51,800 verses (~60,000 with Nanak Prakash) — an ocean

svg.text: Inside: the full narrative of all ten Gurus and Baba Banda Singh Bahadur — the battles,
the sakhis, the shaheedis — gathered from the earlier sources into one continuous epic.
Why it matters: this is the text of daily katha in gurdwaras worldwide — including the
nightly katha at Sri Harmandir Sahib. For nearly two centuries, most Sikhs have
received their history through it. Almost every sakhi you heard growing up
was routed through Suraj Prakash.
➜ Start here: don’t read it — listen. Daily Suraj Prakash katha is on every platform.
Entry effort: ●●●○○ via katha · ●●●●● reading the Braj (14-volume steek exists)
ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਸੁਮਾਰਗ ਗ੍ਰੰਥ · Prem Sumarag
“The True Path of Love” — the rahitnama of the ideal Khalsa society

g.text: Punjabi prose, Gurmukhi — far easier than the poetic granths
10 chapters, from personal rahit to statecraft
a whole civilisation imagined under Akal Purakh’s sovereignty

svg.text: Inside: a complete blueprint of Khalsa life — initiation, daily discipline, birth, naming,
marriage (anand rites), death; then economy, justice, and the model of a benevolent
Khalsa state. Strikingly forward on gender: women receive pahul, girls are educated,
and widows may remarry with honour.
Why it matters: it shows the Khalsa imagining a whole society — not just a code of
conduct but courts, markets and kingship under the Timeless One.
➜ Start here: the life-cycle chapters (birth, anand karaj, death) — readable in an afternoon.
Entry effort: ●●○○○ (prose; translations and studies available)
ਸੌ ਸਾਖੀ · Sau Sakhi (Guru Ratan Mal)
“The Hundred Stories” — the words and prophecies of the Tenth Master

g.text: the words of Guru Gobind Singh Ji
Bhai Ram Kuar Ji, of the house of Baba Buddha Ji (later Bhai Gurbakhsh Singh)
Braj prose with Punjabi verse · 100 sakhis

svg.text: Inside: one hundred sakhis of Guru Gobind Singh Ji — episodes, rahit teachings, and the
famous prophecies of the Khalsa’s coming sovereignty (raj karega khalsa).
Why it matters: through the darkest persecution this book was hope in manuscript
form — the shaheeds of the ghallugharas went to their deaths certain the Panth would
rise. It fired the Namdhari movement under Baba Ram Singh, and the British monitored
its prophecies — an empire, nervous about a book.
➜ Start here: after the others — the prophecies land with more force once you know the history.
Entry effort: ●●●○○
ਗੁਪਤ ਬਾਣੀਆਂ · The rare banis kept by the dals
ਉਗ੍ਰਦੰਤੀ Ugardanti — 6 chhands from the Patna Sahib sarup; given by the Guru so the weak find strength to fight tyranny; in Nihang & Namdhari nitnem ·
ਸ੍ਰੀ ਭਗੌਤੀ ਅਸਤੋਤ੍ਰ Sri Bhagauti Astotra (Sri Sahib ji ki Ustati) — praise of the Divine-Sword, in the Buddha Dal’s puratan Khalsa Nitnem gutkas ·
ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਕਵਚ Brahm Kavach — the protective “armour” bani of the Sarbloh tradition, recited by Nihang Singhs before battle ·
ਆਰਤੀ ਆਰਤਾ Arati-Arata — the full puratan evening prayer with Guru Gobind Singh Ji’s verses, sung nightly at Hazur Sahib and in the dals.
ਰਾਹ · A Suggested Path In

g.text: Das Gur Katha — all ten Gurus

svg.text: Gurbani is the ocean; these granths are the rivers of the Panth’s memory flowing around it. ਧੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਆਈ ਤਿਨਿ ਸਗਲੀ ਚਿੰਤ ਮਿਟਾਈ॥
